prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on a bright bathroom interior. The image is somewhat soft and hazy. The room has light-colored walls and a yellow-toned floor. Visible bathroom furnishings include a sink or vanity area on the left, a chair near the center-left, shelving on the back wall, and a toilet on the right side. A small rug or mat lies on the floor near the right side of the room.

Near the end of the clip, the scene cuts to a product display against a plain beige background. Two blue plastic bottles with yellow caps are placed side by side on a white surface, likely the top of a toilet tank. The left bottle is labeled as a regular Ty-D-bol product, and the text above it reads: “Ty-D-bol Regular Lemon Fresh Blue with BORAX.” The right bottle is larger and labeled as an economy-size version. The text above it reads: “New Ty-D-bol Economy Size with BORAX.” Both bottles prominently show the Ty-D-bol brand name and “BORAX” on their labels.

The final sampled frame is black, indicating a fade-out or cut to black.
